growth_records.strain: MIT1351 / MIT1352 / MIT1353 (range across the three strains)
growth_records.cite: Becker et al. Co-culture and biogeography of Prochlorococcus and SAR11. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0365-4. [PMC6775983]
growth_records.snippet: All three strains had an initial phase of rapid logarithmic growth (growth rate = 5.0–7.7 d−1; about an order of magnitude faster than that of SAR11) ... all three copiotrophs pulled out of stationary phase and resumed growth (at a rate of
growth_records.notes: Reported initial log-phase growth rate range 5.0-7.7 d-1 for the three copiotrophs collectively (per-strain values not resolved in text). Per-hour: 5.0/24=0.2083 to 7.7/24=0.3208 h-1. Doubling time td=24*ln2/mu: for 5.0 d-1 -> 3.33 h; for 7.7 d-1 -> 2.16 h. Secondary diauxic resumption phase reported at 0.49-0.82 d-1 (=0.0204-0.0342 h-1) when Prochlorococcus entered stationary phase. Range reported jointly, so given as a range rather than a single value; not attributable per-strain. Pro99 numeric composition not stated. No fluxes reported.
